title: Swinney would 'consider' banning US military from Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: John Swinney would 'consider' banning US military aircraft from Prestwick Airport (Image: Archive) First Minister John Swinney has said he would "consider" banning US military aircraft from using Prestwick Airport if it was confirmed they were involved in strikes in the Middle East. Swinney previously said the US-Israeli attack on Iran is "not compatible with the international rules-based system".

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: He told the BBC 's Laura Kuenssberg in an interview set to be broadcast on Sunday that he was seeking clarity from the UK government about the role of US planes using the Scottish Government -owned airport. 'Clear answers' demanded after US military plane stops at Prestwick Sir Keir to update nation on Middle East war as officials meet Cypriot chiefs

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Keir Starmer confirms that US using UK bases amid Middle East conflict He said: "We're seeking clarity from the United Kingdom government about the purpose of American flights that are coming in and out of Prestwick, but we don't have the ability to scrutinise those flights."

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – John Swinney would 'consider' banning US military aircraft from Prestwick Airport
